name: Gypsy John
recipeIngredient: 10 oz (10 squares) semisweet baking chocolate, broken into pieces
1 package (6 oz) semisweet chocolate, broken into bits
recipeInstructions:
Cake:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
2. Line a jelly roll pan with waxed paper and grease it.
3. Sift together the flour, cocoa, baking powder, and salt twice; set aside.
4. In a small mixing bowl, beat eggs with an electric mixer for 5 minutes or until thick and lemon-colored.
5. Slowly beat in sugar, about a tablespoon at a time, until the mixture becomes very thick.
6. Transfer the egg mixture to a large mixing bowl.
7. Beat in water and vanilla. Gradually add the flour mixture, beating until smooth.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly to the corners.
9. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. Loosen the cake from the pan; turn it out onto a rack and remove the waxed paper. Invert the cake and cool completely.
Filling:
1. Combine the baking chocolate pieces and heavy cream in a heavy saucepan.
2. Heat slowly, stirring constantly until the chocolate melts.
3. Transfer to a medium-sized mixing bowl. Stir in rum; chill for 1 to 2 hours.
4. Beat with an electric mixer until stiff and thick.
Assembly:
1. Cut the cooled cake in half crosswise.
2. Place one piece of cake on a small cookie sheet.
3. Spread the chocolate filling evenly over the cake to form a layer about 1-1/2 inches thick.
4. Top with the remaining cake layer and chill for at least 1 hour.
Frosting:
1. Combine corn syrup, hot water, and butter in a small saucepan.
2. Bring to a boil; cook until the butter melts.
3. Add chocolate bits and stir until the chocolate melts.
4. Cool at room temperature.
5. Spread the frosting over the top of the cake and chill until set.
6. Cut the cooled cake into 12 squares and arrange on a decorative plate.
